description_long: This cathedral is constructed from light-colored stone blocks, featuring a long rectangular nave with a steeply pitched, reddish-brown tiled roof. The structure is dominated by a tall, pointed bell tower with arched openings and a sharply conical roof, positioned at one end of the building. The facades are punctuated by tall, narrow, pointed-arch windows, some grouped in threes, and a large circular rose window above the main entrance. The entry portal is set within a pointed arch and flanked by smaller arched windows, with minimal decorative ornamentation. Buttresses line the sides, providing structural support and adding to the vertical emphasis typical of Gothic architecture. The overall appearance is imposing yet austere, with a focus on verticality and geometric forms.
description: A large stone cathedral with a tall pointed bell tower, steep tiled roof, and pointed-arch windows, featuring minimal ornamentation and strong vertical lines.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long nave, stone buttresses, and the entry portal beneath a pointed arch, with the bell tower rising at the far end.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the main facade with a large rose window, three tall pointed-arch windows, and the prominent bell tower to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, displaying the length of the nave, buttresses, and the bell tower at the end.
description_view_3: Rear view showing three tall pointed-arch windows and the steep roof, with the bell tower visible in the background.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: The main facade features minimal ornamentation, with pointed arches, a rose window, and simple stonework. There are no visible statues or elaborate reliefs.
attribute_entry_portal_design: The main entry portal is a pointed arch with a simple, recessed design and no elaborate sculptures or doors visible. It is flanked by tall, narrow windows and topped by a rose window.
